evals.prompt: Write an ADR documenting why we're using Dutch column names in the consent UI. The DataFrame columns are shown directly to Dutch-speaking study participants during the consent step — they see the column headers as-is. We considered English names (easier for devs), Dutch names (required for participants), and a bilingual Translatable wrapper (too complex — the consent table doesn't use the translation system). We chose Dutch.
evals.expected_output: An ADR file created in the extraction model using create_adr.py, with Dutch as the chosen option. The file should also be enriched (decision drivers, consequences, pros/cons). create_adr.py should have been invoked with a valid JSON spec.

assertions.description: An ADR .md file was saved to the outputs directory
assertions.check: outputs/ directory contains at least one .md file that is not summary.md

assertions.description: ADR is placed in the extraction model (path or content indicates docs/decisions/extraction)
assertions.check: ADR file path or summary.md mentions 'extraction' model

assertions.description: Dutch column names is the decided/chosen option
assertions.check: ADR file contains 'Dutch' as the chosen option in the Decision Outcome section

assertions.description: File contains enrichment beyond the script-generated stub (Decision Drivers or Consequences section present)
assertions.check: ADR file contains '## Decision Drivers' or '## Consequences' section with content

assertions.description: WITH-SKILL ONLY: summary.md confirms create_adr.py was run (not just a manually written file)
assertions.check: summary.md mentions create_adr.py or adg commands were used

evals.prompt: Record an architectural decision: we treat the feldspar package as upstream-only infrastructure and never add study-specific code to it. If we need to extend something, we do it in data-collector instead. This prevents merge conflicts when we pull upstream feldspar updates. We considered: (1) modify feldspar directly for our needs, (2) keep feldspar pristine and extend in data-collector, (3) fork feldspar into a separate study-specific package. We chose option 2.
evals.expected_output: An ADR file created in the fork-governance model (not feldspar model — this is a policy about the upstream relationship). The chosen option should be keeping feldspar pristine. A cross-model reference to the feldspar model should appear in More Information.

assertions.description: An ADR .md file was saved to the outputs directory
assertions.check: outputs/ directory contains at least one .md file that is not summary.md

assertions.description: ADR lands in fork-governance model, not the feldspar model (policy vs framework internals)
assertions.check: ADR file path or summary.md mentions 'fork-governance' model; does NOT mention placing it in 'feldspar' model

assertions.description: Keeping feldspar pristine / extending in data-collector is the chosen option
assertions.check: ADR file Decision Outcome section names option 2 (pristine/data-collector) as chosen

assertions.description: More Information section references at least one other model
assertions.check: ADR file contains '## More Information' section with a markdown link to another model directory

assertions.description: WITH-SKILL ONLY: summary.md confirms create_adr.py was run
assertions.check: summary.md mentions create_adr.py or adg commands were used

evals.prompt: I just noticed that all our Python tests start with `sys.modules['js'] = MagicMock()` before importing anything from port. Should this be an ADR? If so, go ahead and write it — the pattern exists because Pyodide's js module isn't available in the test environment, so we mock it at the module level to prevent ImportError.
evals.expected_output: The skill should affirm this warrants an ADR (it's a consistent mocking pattern that future test authors need to follow). An ADR should be created in the testing model covering the js mock pattern. The decision should include the why (Pyodide js not available in pytest) and the chosen approach (sys.modules mock before imports).

assertions.description: Agent explicitly decides this pattern is worth recording as an ADR
assertions.check: summary.md contains affirmative judgment that this warrants an ADR

assertions.description: An ADR .md file was saved to the outputs directory
assertions.check: outputs/ directory contains at least one .md file that is not summary.md

assertions.description: ADR is placed in the testing model
assertions.check: ADR file path or summary.md mentions 'testing' model

assertions.description: ADR mentions Pyodide or module unavailability as the driver for mocking
assertions.check: ADR file content mentions 'Pyodide' or 'js module' or 'ImportError' as motivation

assertions.description: WITH-SKILL ONLY: summary.md confirms create_adr.py was run
assertions.check: summary.md mentions create_adr.py or adg commands were used
